DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT PAUL NOUNOU'S MOTION FOR ORDER FINDING ATTORNEY ABUSED DISCOVERY PROCESS, ORDER TO COMPEL RESPONSES, ORDER TO APPOINT A DISCOVERY REFEREE IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF RAY RICHARDSON SHALL BE RECONVENED AT A TIME AND PLACE MUTUALLY CONVENIENT TO THE PARTIES. THE MEET AND CONFER EFFORTS MADE ON THE RECORD DURING THE DEPOSITION WERE SUFFICIENT. SEE STEWART V. COLONIAL WESTERN AGENCY, INC. (2001) 87 CAL.APP.4TH 1006, 1015-17; TOWNSEND V. SUPERIOR COURT (1998) 61 CAL.APP.4TH 1431, 1438. THE OBJECTIONS TO THE QUESTIONS SET FORTH IN THE DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT ARE OVERRULED. ATTORNEY KAPLAN IS ORDERED TO LIMIT HIS OBJECTIONS TO THE FORM OF THE QUESTION ONLY AND REFRAIN FROM ADDITIONAL REMARKS DIRECTED EITHER AT OPPOSING COUNSEL OR HIS OWN CLIENT. IF AN OBJECTION IS BASED ON PRIVILEGE, IT IS TO BE STATED CLEARLY AND SUCCINCTLY AND IF THE WITNESS IS INSTRUCTED NOT TO ANSWER, THE INSTRUCTION IS TO BE STATED CLEARLY AND SUCCINCTLY WITHOUT ADDITIONAL COMMENTARY. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR THE APPOINTMENT OF A DISCOVERY REFEREE IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. SHOULD ATTORNEY KAPLAN'S CONDUCT AT THIS DEPOSITION CONTINUE, OR BE REPEATED AT ANY SUBSEQUENT DEPOSITION IN THIS CASE, THE COURT WILL ENTERTAIN A RENEWED REQUEST FOR THE APPOINTMENT OF A DISCOVERY REFEREE WITH THE COSTS OF THE REFEREE TO BE BORNE BY THE PLAINTIFF. SEE CAL. CODE CIV. PROC. 639, 645.1. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: NOAH LEBOWITZ, CLERK: FELICIA GREEN, NOT REPORTED, DEPT. 302.

Defendant Paul Nounou'S Motion For Order Finding Attorney Abused Discovery Process, Order To Compel Responses, Order To Appoint A Discovery Referre
Off Calendar Aug-08-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: The Court adopts the tentative ruling on Defendant Paul Nounou'S Motion For Order Finding Attorney Abused Discovery Process, Order To Compel Responses, Order To Appoint A Discovery Referee as follows:

Defendant's motion is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part without prejudice. The deposition of Plaintiff Ray Richardson shall be reconvened at a time and place mutually convenient to the parties. The meet and confer efforts made on the record during the deposition were sufficient. See Stewart v. Colonial Western Agency, Inc. (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 1006, 1015-17; Townsend v. Superior Court (1998) 61 Cal.App.4th 1431, 1438. The objections to the questions set forth in the Defendant's separate statement are overruled. Attorney Kaplan is ordered to limit his objections to the form of the question only and refrain from additional remarks directed either at opposing counsel or his own client. If an objection is based on privilege, it is to be stated clearly and succinctly and if the witness is instructed not to answer, the instruction is to be stated clearly and succinctly without additional commentary. Defendant's request for the appointment of a discovery referee is DENIED without prejudice. Should attorney Kaplan's conduct at this deposition continue, or be repeated at any subsequent deposition in this case, the Court will entertain a renewed request for the appointment of a discovery referee with the costs of the referee to be borne by the Plaintiff. See Cal. Code Civ. Proc. 639, 645.1. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is DENIED. Defendant to prepare proposed order. Judge Pro Tem: Noah Lebowitz, Clerk: Felicia Green, Not reported, Dept. 302.
